This script uses Spearman's rank correlation to determine whether there is a monotonic relationship between membrane resistance (Ri) and coupling index (CS_EC). The coupling index was determined by comparing the fluorescence intensity of the processes of the patched cells to the fluorescence intensity of the underlying endothelial cell. Normality was assessed using the Shapiro-Wilk test. Since Ri was not normally distributed (W = 0.50, p < 0.001), Spearman's rank correlation was used.

H0: There is no monotonic relationship between Ri and Coupling Index (ρ = 0)
H1: There is a monotonic relationship between Ri and Coupling Index (ρ ≠ 0)

# load data
df       <- read.csv("Coupling_Matrix_Culture_Ri.csv")

# normality check
check_normality <- function(x, name) {
  sw <- shapiro.test(x)
  cat(sprintf("%s – Shapiro-Wilk: W = %.3f, p = %.3f\n", name, sw$statistic, sw$p.value))
  par(mfrow = c(1, 2))
  hist(x, main = paste("Histogram –", name), xlab = name, col = "steelblue", border = "white")
  qqnorm(x, main = paste("QQ-Plot –", name))
  qqline(x, col = "red")
  par(mfrow = c(1, 1))
}

check_normality(df$Ri,    "Ri")
check_normality(df$CS_EC, "Coupling Index")

# spearman's rank correlation
result <- cor.test(df$Ri, df$CS_EC, method = "spearman", exact = FALSE)
cat(sprintf("\nSpearman rho = %.2f, p = %.5f\n", result$estimate, result$p.value))
